allured.description:
A creature that is allured to another creature will do whatever they tell them to. They are totally loyal to that other creature and also @Identifier[condition:bound]{bound} to them. This is a @Wiki[Category:Control effects]{control effect}, but it is not forcing actions directly. Rather, an allured creature wants to take action, is does so of their own volition. In a "regular" @Identifier[condition:bound]{binding}, the creature is aware they are being @Wiki[Category:Control effects]{controlled} and can attempt to thwart the confines of their tether. But an allured creature is not aware of the effect that's been placed on them. It is more akin to being insane, blurring reality and shaping it to what they believe to be true.

This rare and dangerous type of @Wiki[Category:Control effects]{control} is usually employed by @Identifier[creature:nymph]{nymphs}, @UUID[Compendium.teriock.tables.RollTable.65fbed36e42b583a]{vampires}, and other manipulative and generally evil creatures.
allured.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.allured

anosmatic.description: If you are anosmatic then you have no sense of smell and cannot use any @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities} that would require one.
anosmatic.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.anosmatic

asleep.description: A creature that's asleep is unconscious, but not necessarily at 0 or fewer @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}. Unless otherwise specified, a creature that's asleep will @Identifier[keyword:awaken]{awaken} if they take any damage (that doesn't reduce them to 0 or fewer @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}) or if they are within range of something @Wiki[Category:Loud effects]{loud}. If an effect specifies a range it can be heard at, it is assumed that half that range is the range it can wake sleeping creatures at.
asleep.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.asleep

blind.description: If you are blind, then you cannot see anything and any @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attack} you make against another creature is done with @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage}.
blind.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.blind

bound.description: If you are bound to another group, concept, or being, you cannot intentionally let them come to harm through action or inaction. This is a @Wiki[Category:Control effects]{control effect}.
bound.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.bound

burned.description: A burned creature is @Identifier[keyword:incapable]{incapable} of @Identifier[core:healing]{healing} itself with @Wiki[Category:Regeneration effects]{regeneration effects}.
burned.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.burned

charmed.description: A creature that is charmed has their allegiances switched. They will fight their former allies and defend their former foes. This does not change the alignment of the creature, only their loyalties. A charmed creature is visibly so, they have a glazed and bloodshot look in their eyes.
charmed.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.charmed

criticallyWounded.description: Creatures become critically wounded when they are 1 @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} or @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} above half their maximum @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} or @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} below zero but also have at least zero @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} or @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} respectively. This is 1 @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} or @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} above the threshold for being dead. A creature that's critically wounded cannot be @Identifier[keyword:awaken]{awakened}.
criticallyWounded.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.criticallyWounded

dead.description:
Creatures become dead if they are half their maximum @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} or @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} below zero @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} or @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} respectively. They cannot have their @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} or @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} reduced below this value. If a creature is @Identifier[condition:down]{down} and at negative @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} for more than 10 minutes, they die.

When a creature dies and is @Wiki[Category:Revival effects]{revived}, they release control over anything that is no longer on their person, freeing up the corresponding @Identifier[core:presence]{presence} in the process. This includes @Pack[teriock.equipment]{equipment} that is no longer equipped, mounts that no longer with them, and so on. They also must pull from the @Identifier[core:death-bag]{death bag}.
dead.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.dead

deaf.description: If you are deaf then you have no sense of hearing and cannot use any @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities} that would require one.
deaf.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.deaf

defyingDeath.description:
If you are defying death, make an @Identifier[core:presence]{UNP} @Identifier[core:feat-interaction]{feat save} every time you would take @Identifier[core:damage]{damage}. The DC of this save is equal to 6 plus the number of damage dice that would be dealt. If you succeed, you take no @Identifier[core:damage]{damage} from that source. If you fail, you @Identifier[condition:dead]{die}.

While defying death, you also @Identifier[keyword:resistance]{resist} @Identifier[condition:dead]{death} and @Identifier[condition:unconscious]{unconsciousness}.
defyingDeath.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.defyingDeath

disguised.description: A creature that's disguised is not @Identifier[condition:transformed]{transformed}, but still appears differently than their natural form. Any creature can detect if someone is disguised using @Identifier[ability:check-disguise]{Check Disguise}.
disguised.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.disguised

down.description: A creature is down if it is @Identifier[condition:unconscious]{unconscious} or @Identifier[condition:dead]{dead}. The act of going from @Identifier[rule:up]{up} to down is called "dropping". If a creature is down and at negative @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP} for more than 10 minutes, they @Identifier[condition:dead]{die}. When a creature goes down, they are usually also knocked @Identifier[condition:prone]{prone}.
down.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.down

dueling.description:
Creatures that are dueling each other are @Wiki[Category:Spiritual effects]{spiritually} linked to each other in a state that's slightly out of phase with everyone else. This doesn't have any inherent mechanical effects in and of itself, but it implies a structure for how those @Pack[teriock.creatures]{creatures} interact with each other and the world around them that may be interacted with by @Wiki[Category:Duel modifying effects]{duel modifying effects} or other @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities}. The term "in a duel" is often used to refer to a creature that is dueling. This does not mean that the "duel" must have been started by @Identifier[ability:duel]{Duel}.

If a creature that is dueling becomes incapacitated, then they are considered to have "lost" the duel. If a dueling creature incapacitates themselves, they are considered to have "forfeited" the duel which in turn also makes them count as "losing". If a dueling creature @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} a creature that they are not dueling, that is also considered to be "forfeiting". If a creature is ever the only one remaining in a given duel, the duel ends and they are considered to have "won" the duel.

Duels typically have a "range" of 30 feet. This means that if any creature that is dueling @Identifier[ability:move]{Moves} more than 30 feet away from any other creature that they are dueling with, they are considered to have "forfeited" the duel.

The dueling state is not visible in the Material Realm, but there is a very slight tether that can be seen in the @Identifier[condition:ethereal]{Ethereal Realm}. There are no restrictions on what realm a duel can take place in, but all participants must be within the same realm. Any participant that leaves that realm is considered to have gone out of range and therefore "lost the duel".
dueling.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.dueling

encumbered.description:
An encumbered creature can be either lightly encumbered or heavily encumbered. The consequences are as follows.

Lightly Encumbered
The creature's @Identifier[core:movement-speed]{movement speed} is reduced by 10.
Heavily Encumbered
The creature is @Identifier[condition:slowed]{slowed}. The creature also has @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage} on @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} and @Identifier[core:movement]{MOV} @Identifier[core:feat-interaction]{feat saves}.
encumbered.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.encumbered

ethereal.description:
If a creature is ethereal, it is in the Ethereal Realm. This is the domain of spirits and does not take kindly to those with a physical form. Ethereal beings cannot typically be interacted with nor sensed by material (non-ethereal) ones. The main exception is that if an ethereal being passes within 5 feet of a material one, the material creature feels a strange unnatural chill pass over them.

If a creature that's ethereal is not innately a spirit, then they still have a size and shape while ethereal, but it is blurry and transparent. They cannot pass through material (non-ethereal) walls or objects. However, they also cannot push, pull, grab, nor interact with material objects in any way. It is as though the ethereal creature has no material strength whatsoever.

If you go @Identifier[condition:down]{down} while ethereal, you @Identifier[condition:dead]{die}, become a @Identifier[condition:wisping]{wisp} and your body dissipates. Any being that has a physical form and that isn't being kept ethereal by some effect will manifest back into the Material Realm at dawn. They remember what they experienced while ethereal, but the details and order of events is a little jumbled and hazy.

There is a slight whooshing sound like a gust of wind whenever a creature enters or exits the Ethereal Realm.
ethereal.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.ethereal

frenzied.description: While frenzied, you will not leave combat willingly and are @Identifier[keyword:inept]{inept} at not killing anyone who opposes you. This includes anyone who tries to stop you through actions or words. You are also @Identifier[keyword:resistant]{resistant} to being @Identifier[condition:frightened]{frightened}.
frenzied.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.frenzied

frightened.description: A frightened creature cannot make @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} against nor intentionally @Identifier[ability:move]{get closer to} the source of their fear.
frightened.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.frightened

frozen.description: A frozen creature cannot use any @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities} or be affected by anything until this condition is cleared. They have no perception of the world around them or the passage of time at all. Any other ongoing @UUID[Compendium.teriock.rules.JournalEntry.condition0000000]{conditions} or other ongoing effects are paused while this condition is in place. If a creature is no longer frozen, time immediately catches up to them. They age the amount of time that they were frozen and any effects with a finite duration that would have ended do so.
frozen.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.frozen

goaded.description: A creature that's goaded has @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage} on @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} against creatures other than ones that caused it to be goaded.
goaded.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.goaded

grounded.description: If a creature is grounded then their flying, climbing, and leaping (both vertical and horizontal) @Identifier[core:movement-speed]{speed adjustments} are set to "none".
grounded.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.grounded

hacked.description:
A creature that's hacked is one suffering from @Identifier[damage:hack]{hack damage}. Hack damage does not reduce @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}. Instead, it applies different @UUID[Compendium.teriock.rules.JournalEntry.condition0000000]{conditions} or effects to creatures depending on the part of their body being hacked. Hack damage will always have some body part specified.

Arm Hacks
1st Arm Hack: @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{Attacks} you make with handheld @Wiki[Category:Item delivery abilities]{items}, @Wiki[Category:Missile delivery abilities]{missiles}, or your @Wiki[Category:Hand delivery abilities]{hands} are done with @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage}. And you cannot make any @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} requiring two hands (such as those with @Identifier[property:two-handed]{two-handed} @UUID[Compendium.teriock.tables.RollTable.63e9a3bd88895816]{weapons}). You also cannot execute @Wiki[Category:Somatic component abilities]{somatic abilities} unless both your hands are free.
2nd Arm Hack: You now cannot @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attack}, @Wiki[Category:Block abilities]{block}, nor use any other @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities} with handheld @Wiki[Category:Item delivery abilities]{items}, @Wiki[Category:Missile delivery abilities]{missiles}, or your @Wiki[Category:Hand delivery abilities]{hands} at all. You also cannot execute @Wiki[Category:Somatic component abilities]{somatic abilities} at all.
Leg Hacks
1st Leg Hack: You are @Identifier[condition:slowed]{slowed} and @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} you make with your @Identifier[body:foot]{feet} are done with @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage}.
2nd Leg Hack: You are @Identifier[condition:immobilized]{immobilized} and cannot make @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} with your @Identifier[body:foot]{feet}.
Vitals Hacks
The effects of Vitals Hacks have even more granularity. Vitals Hacks will always have a more specific body part specified and apply effects accordingly.
Vitals Ear Hack: You are @Identifier[condition:deaf]{deaf}.
Vitals Eye Hack: You are @Identifier[condition:blind]{blind}.
Vitals Nose Hack: You are @Identifier[condition:anosmatic]{anosmatic}.
Vitals Mouth Hack: You are @Identifier[condition:mute]{mute} and cannot make @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} with your @Identifier[equipment:mouth]{mouth}.
Body Hacks
You are @Identifier[condition:immobile]{immobile}.

Despite not reducing @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}, hack damage can be @Identifier[core:healing]{healed}. @Identifier[core:healing]{Healing} hack damage does not typically consume @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{hit dice}. However, one @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{hit die} worth of @Identifier[core:healing]{healing} only mends one hack. Each type of vitals hack must be @Identifier[core:healing]{healed} independently. Arms and legs must receive an amount of @Identifier[core:healing]{healing} equal to the amount of hacks they've taken in order to be fully @Identifier[core:healing]{healed}.
hacked.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.hacked

hidden.description:
When you are hidden, you cannot be seen. This condition is always associated with some hidden DC it takes to see you. Unless otherwise noted, it's your @Wiki[Core:Passive Attributes]{passive} @Identifier[core:sneak]{sneak} (10 + 2 × @Identifier[core:sneak]{SNK}). Other creatures will only be able to see you (or know that you're there) if they use @Identifier[ability:perceive]{some relevant ability} that lets them do so, if their @Wiki[Core:Passive Attributes]{passive} @Identifier[core:perception]{perception} (10 + 2 × @Identifier[core:perception]{PER}) is greater than your hidden DC, or if enabled to by some other effect. In most cases, a creature trying to see you will have some check which must pass your hidden DC.

While hidden, if someone knows where you are but is unable to see you, any @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} they make against you are done with @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage}. Similarly, any @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} you make against someone that can't see you are done with @Identifier[core:advantage]{advantage}. @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{Attacking} causes you to no longer be hidden.

While hidden, you must not @Identifier[ability:move]{Move} faster than your @Identifier[core:movement-speed]{hidden speed}. Any other @Wiki[Category:Movement effects]{movement effects} require a DC 15 @Identifier[core:sneak]{SNK} @Identifier[core:feat-interaction]{feat save} to remain hidden.
hidden.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.hidden

immobilized.description: If a creature is immobilized then all their @Identifier[core:movement-speed]{movement speed adjustments} are set to "none" and they are @Identifier[keyword:incapable]{incapable} of using @Wiki[Category:Movement effects]{movement effects} or otherwise @Identifier[ability:move]{moving} of their own volition. They also automatically fail all @Wiki[Category:MOV save abilities]{MOV saves}.
immobilized.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.immobilized

invisible.description: An invisible creature cannot be seen. It is as though the creature is @Identifier[condition:hidden]{hidden}, but movement does not typically break invisibility and cannot be seen with @Identifier[ability:perceive]{Perceive}.
invisible.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.invisible

lighted.description: A lighted creature cannot make @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} against at least 1 other creature, as specified by the source of the effect.
lighted.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.lighted

meleeDodging.description: If you are melee dodging then the threshold required for melee @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} to hit you is increased by 2. This stacks with other modifications to being hit. So, for instance, the threshold to hit an item you wield is @Identifier[core:armor-class]{AC} + 3 - @Identifier[core:block-interaction]{BV} + 2.
meleeDodging.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.meleeDodging

missileDodging.description: If you are missile dodging then the threshold required for ranged @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} to hit you is increased by 2. This stacks with other modifications to being hit. So, for instance, the threshold to hit an item you wield is @Identifier[core:armor-class]{AC} + 3 - @Identifier[core:block-interaction]{BV} + 2.
missileDodging.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.missileDodging

mute.description: If you are mute then you cannot speak nor use any @Wiki[Category:Verbal component abilities]{abilities that require verbal components}.
mute.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.mute

odorless.description: An odorless creature cannot be detected by scent.
odorless.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.odorless

paralyzed.description: A creature that is paralyzed is completely physically inert. They are @Identifier[condition:immobilized]{immobilized}, cannot use any @Wiki[Category:Active abilities]{active}, @Wiki[Category:Reactive abilities]{reactive}, or @Wiki[Category:Slow abilities]{slow abilities}, and they automatically fail all @Wiki[Category:STR save abilities]{STR save abilities}.
paralyzed.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.paralyzed

poisoned.description: A creature that is poisoned has a recurring @Wiki[Category:Effects]{effect} that applies to them at the start of their turn. By default, the @Wiki[Category:Effects]{effect} is [[/roll 1d6]] @Identifier[damage:toxic]{toxic damage}. The effect could be something else specified in the source of the poisoned condition.
poisoned.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.poisoned

prone.description: A creature that's prone may only @Identifier[ability:move]{Move} by @Identifier[core:movement-speed]{crawling}, unless it uses @Identifier[ability:stand-up]{Stand Up} and thereby stops being prone. A prone creature also has @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage} with all @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} and @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attacks} made against them from within 5 feet of them have @Identifier[core:advantage]{advantage}. Otherwise, an @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attack} made against a prone creature has @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage}.
prone.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.prone

ruined.description: A ruined body is one that has been damaged beyond the point of normal @Identifier[core:healing]{healing} or @Wiki[Category:Revival effects]{reviving}. If a creature's body is ruined, then it must be reconstructed before the creature can be brought back from @Identifier[condition:dead]{the dead}. This is most often done through @Identifier[ability:summon-the-dead]{Summon the Dead}.
ruined.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.ruined

silenced.description: A creature that is silenced is @Identifier[condition:mute]{mute} and @Identifier[keyword:incapable]{incapable} of non-@Identifier[power:basic-abilities]{basic} @Identifier[core:attack-interaction]{attack} and @Wiki[Category:Feat abilities]{feat} @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities}.
silenced.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.silenced

silent.description: A silent creature cannot be detected by sound.
silent.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.silent

slowed.description: If a creature is slowed then all their @Identifier[core:movement-speed]{movement speed adjustments} are lowered one step, all @Wiki[Core:Special Movements]{special movements} they make cost twice as much movement, and they make @Wiki[Category:MOV save abilities]{MOV saves} with @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage}.
slowed.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.slowed

snared.description: If a creature is snared then they are @Identifier[condition:immobile]{immobile} and cannot be @Wiki[Category:Movement effects]{moved} by themself nor by others. They also have @Identifier[core:disadvantage]{disadvantage} on all @Wiki[Category:MOV save abilities]{MOV saves}.
snared.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.snared

suffocating.description: A creature that's suffocating must spend a @Wiki[Core:Hit Dice]{hit die} at the start of each of their turns or they drop to 0 @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}. This does not restore @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}. If they have 0 or fewer @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}, then they instead @Identifier[condition:dead]{die}.
suffocating.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.suffocating

terrored.description: A terrored creature @Identifier[keyword:resistance]{resists} all @Wiki[Category:Healing effects]{healing} and @Wiki[Category:Revival effects]{revival effects}.
terrored.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.terrored

transformed.description:
If a creature is transformed then they are in a different form than their original form. Transformations can have different levels of strength.

Minor Transformation
A minor transformation is a transformation that is not very strong and is usually temporary. It can be reverted with a @Identifier[ability:treeform-ball]{Treeform Ball}, a @Identifier[ability:force-revert]{Force Revert}, or with a @Identifier[ability:self-revert]{Self Revert} (DC 6 @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} @Identifier[core:feat-interaction]{feat save}). Minor transformations do not change creatures' @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} nor maximum @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP}. Minor transformations cannot be "layered". That is, if a creature is minorly transformed into one form, and then minorly transformed into another, reverting from the second form puts them back into their original state. They do not revert into the first form they transformed into. Creatures are not @Identifier[core:proficiency-bonus]{proficient} in @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities} they gain from being minorly transformed.
Full Transformation
A full transformation is stronger than a minor transformation. It cannot be reverted with a @Identifier[ability:treeform-ball]{Treeform Ball} or a @Identifier[ability:force-revert]{Force Revert}. But it can be reverted with a @Identifier[ability:self-revert]{Self Revert} (DC 12 @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} @Identifier[core:feat-interaction]{feat save}). Full transformations change creatures' @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} from that of their original form to the one they're transformed into at a rate of 1 per month. It takes the same amount of time to recover if reverted. A creature's maximum @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} does not change as a result of a full transformation until their @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} has fully changed. Similarly to minor transformations, full transforms can't be layered on top of each other. However, a fully transformed creature can then be minorly transformed. If that minor transformation is reverted, the creature then returns to their fully transformed state. Creatures are not @Identifier[core:proficiency-bonus]{proficient} in @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities} they gain from being fully transformed.
Greater Transformation
A greater transformation is even stronger than a full transformation. It can be reverted with a @Identifier[ability:self-revert]{Self Revert} (DC 18 @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} @Identifier[core:feat-interaction]{feat save}). Greater transformations immediately change creature's @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} and maximum @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} to that of whatever they're transformed into. The original @Identifier[core:intelligence]{INT} and maximum @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{MP} are instantly restored upon reverting from a greater transformation. As with minor and full transformations, greater transformations do not layer on top of each other. However, a full or minor transformation can be layered on top of a greater in the same way that a minor transformation can be layered on a full one.
transformed.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.transformed

unconscious.description: A creature that's unconscious is incapacitated. They are @Identifier[condition:down]{down}, but @Identifier[rule:alive]{alive}. They have no awareness of their surroundings, and can't use any non-passive @Pack[teriock.abilities]{abilities}. They also automatically fail any @Wiki[Category:Feat abilities]{feat saves}. A creature that's unconscious with 0 or greater @Identifier[core:hit-points-and-mana-points]{HP}, but not @Identifier[condition:asleep]{asleep} will @Identifier[keyword:awaken]{awaken} after 1 hour.
unconscious.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.unconscious

wisping.description: When a creature is wisping, their spirit has separated from their body. They have the stats of a @Identifier[creature:wisp]{wisp} and exist in a dreamlike state. They cannot interact with their surroundings and typically have no other items nor abilities. Wisps are always @Identifier[condition:ethereal]{Ethereal}.
wisping.name: TERIOCK.STATUSES.Conditions.wisping
